frameworks.name: probabilistic mental simulation
frameworks.one_line: approximate noisy simulation of physical dynamics to predict outcomes

frameworks.name: feature/abstraction-based reasoning
frameworks.one_line: discrete heuristic features replace full simulation for efficiency

frameworks.name: blended simulation-abstraction model
frameworks.one_line: linear path projection substitutes simulation under specific conditions

frameworks.name: mental physics engine
frameworks.one_line: flexible engine adaptively invokes cheaper abstractions when useful

core_question: How do people combine probabilistic physical simulation with abstract features when reasoning about everyday physical events?
key_assumptions: physical reasoning can rely on approximate probabilistic simulation
abstractions trade fidelity for computational efficiency
linear path projection is the relevant abstraction for falling-ball judgments
manipulations: availability of straight-line paths between ball and target
simulation time held fixed across conditions
ball trajectory geometry in contact judgments
key_findings: response times are longer when straight-line paths are unavailable even with fixed simulation time
people misjudge ball trajectories consistent with linear path projection
interpretations: pure-simulation accounts are insufficient given RT pattern
people use a flexible mental physics engine that adaptively invokes efficient abstractions
